entries.title: President Trump’s face could appear on a $250 bill and the U.S. agrees to a deal with Kenya to establish an Ebola quarantine center for Americans. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:

A $5 million settlement issued at the end of the Biden administration became the focus of a lobbying campaign by the billionaire Winklevoss twins

Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer said she won’t seek the presidency in 2028, taking one of the Democratic Party’s high-profile stars out of the running

The White House Correspondents’ Association is discussing holding a do-over of its annual dinner in the coming weeks, after an assassination attempt targeting President Trump abruptly ended the event in April
President Trump said Wednesday that Iran was miscalculating if it thought he would soften his position to avoid a prolonged standoff with Tehran
The Trump administration announced a new green card policy Friday that, if carried out as intended, would force most applicants for permanent residency to leave the country and apply from abroad
For more than a year, Republican senators had largely been deferential to President Trump‘s wishes. This week, they revolted over a Trump endorsement and his $1.776 billion “anti-weaponization fund.”
Sen. John Cornyn of Texas becomes a member of the swelling YOLO Caucus after losing his primary against a Trump-backed opponent. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
President Trump and Democrats rarely find themselves in alignment. Yet both sides wanted the same outcome in Tuesday’s Texas Senate primary runoff election.
The balance of power in the Senate is on the line in the 2026 elections. Our tracker digs into the most competitive and emerging Senate battlegrounds.
State Attorney General Ken Paxton clinched the Texas Republican nomination for U.S. Senate, defeating longtime Sen. John Cornyn
Candidates’ whiskers have emerged as a divisive issue in the race for California governor, reflecting a new bearded age in American politics.
